open_positions.meaning: Bet that Chicago daily high on Feb 22 will be >= 32F
Bet that New York daily high on Feb 22 will be < 38F

scan_for_edges.description: Scan all 6 cities for edge opportunities. Returns edges with ensemble probability, market price, edge%, and suggested contracts.
scan_for_edges.when_to_run: At each scheduled check time. This is your primary action.

edge_single_city.description: Deep edge analysis for one city.

buy.description: Buy contracts. SIDE is 'yes' or 'no'. DOLLAR_AMOUNT is how much to spend. --price is price per contract in cents.
buy.notes: The scan command provides suggested_contracts and the market price. Use those values.

sell.description: Close an open position. SIDE must match the open position's side. --price is the sell price in cents.
sell.notes: Use when you want to take profit or cut losses before settlement.

settle.description: Settle a paper position after the market event has occurred. OUTCOME is 'won' or 'lost'.
settle.notes: Use this after the trading day ends and you know the actual high temperature. If your bet was correct, use 'won'. If wrong, use 'lost'.

side_meaning.yes: Betting the daily high will be >= the threshold temperature
side_meaning.no: Betting the daily high will be < the threshold temperature

when_to_buy.rule: Only buy when scan reports an edge >= 8%
when_to_buy.sizing: Use the suggested_contracts from scan output. This is quarter-Kelly capped at 5% of balance.
when_to_buy.price: Use the market price from scan output as --price
when_to_buy.auto_approve_rule: If edge > 15% and suggested cost < 5% of balance, execute immediately without hesitation.
when_to_buy.review_rule: If edge is 8-15%, execute but note it as a standard-confidence trade.

when_to_sell.rule: Sell an open position early if any of these are true
conditions: A new scan shows the edge has flipped (your side is now unfavorable)
You can lock in profit: current market price for your side is significantly higher than your entry price
The forecast has shifted dramatically against your position since entry
when_to_sell.how: Run edge for that city. If the ensemble now disagrees with your position, sell at current market price.

when_to_settle.rule: Settle positions after the market event date has passed
how_to_determine_outcome: The date in the ticker (e.g. 22FEB26) is the event day
After that day ends, check what the actual high temperature was at the NWS station
Compare actual high to the T{n} threshold in the ticker
If you held YES and actual high >= threshold: settle as 'won'
If you held YES and actual high < threshold: settle as 'lost'
If you held NO and actual high < threshold: settle as 'won'
If you held NO and actual high >= threshold: settle as 'lost'
when_to_settle.nws_check: Look up the actual daily high from NWS for the station. Settlement source is the NWS CLI (Climatological Report) released the morning after.

schedule.description: Run scans aligned to GFS model update availability. All times US Eastern.

check_times_et.reason: GFS 00Z run available. Scan for tomorrow's edges. Place initial orders.
GFS 06Z run available. Update analysis. Adjust if forecast shifted.
PRIME WINDOW. Biggest edges. Market hasn't reacted to overnight models yet. Scan and trade aggressively.
GFS 12Z available. Final model update before afternoon high.
GFS 18Z available. Start analyzing next day's markets.

settlement_check.time: 08:00-10:00 ET the morning after event day
settlement_check.action: Check NWS actual high temperature for each city with open positions. Settle all expired positions.

risk_rules: Never risk more than 5% of total balance on a single position
If total balance drops 25% from peak ($750 from $1000 start), pause trading for 48 hours and review
Do not hold more than 3 open positions in the same city on the same day
Do not buy if edge < 8% — the model error likely exceeds the edge
If spread is > 10 cents, recalculate edge net of spread or use limit orders
Always use --json flag so you can parse output programmatically

managing_existing_positions.description: You have inherited 2 open positions. Here is how to handle them.
steps: 1. Check what day it is relative to the position dates (22FEB26 = February 22, 2026)
2. If today is BEFORE the event date: run 'edge CHI' and 'edge NY' to see if your positions still have edge. If edge has flipped against you, sell early.
3. If today IS the event date: monitor. You can sell during the day if you want to lock in profit/cut losses, or hold to settlement.
4. If today is AFTER the event date: look up actual high temperatures and settle each position as won or lost.

position_1.your_bet: Chicago high on Feb 22 will be >= 32F

position_2.your_bet: New York high on Feb 22 will be < 38F

workflow_loop: 1. Run: {base_command} positions --json → check open positions and balance
2. For each open position past its event date → look up actual NWS high temp → run settle command
3. Run: {base_command} scan --json → look for new edges
4. For each edge >= 8% in scan results → run buy command using suggested_contracts and market price
5. For each open position where edge has flipped → run sell command
7. Wait until next scheduled check time
